stem: Having chosen not to rely on any runtime mitigation, a team wants to rigorously correct measurement (readout) error in their SamplerV2 counts — not merely post-select. Which do-it-yourself technique fits the raw per-shot data the Sampler provides?

options.text: Characterize readout with calibration circuits to build an assignment (confusion) matrix, then apply its inverse to the measured counts (e.g. a matrix-based method such as M3)
Raise `sampler.options.resilience_level` until the readout error disappears
Apply Zero-Noise Extrapolation directly to the bitstrings
Turn on gate twirling, which mathematically inverts the readout confusion matrix

explanation.correct: Readout error is a classical relabeling of measured bits, captured by an assignment/confusion matrix measured from calibration circuits (prepare each basis state, see what you read). Inverting (or least-squares inverting) that matrix corrects the counts — the approach used by tools like mthree (M3). The Sampler's raw counts are exactly the input such a method needs.

distractors.B: SamplerV2 has no `resilience_level`; that Estimator option cannot be set here, and it would not build a readout-calibration matrix anyway.
distractors.C: Zero-Noise Extrapolation mitigates errors in *expectation values* by scaling gate noise (an Estimator technique); it is not defined on raw sampled bitstrings.
distractors.D: Twirling randomizes coherent gate/measurement noise into a simpler stochastic form; it does not measure or invert the readout confusion matrix.
